NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 12 actions returned the board to a state already seen 12 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 12 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"Step: {current_frame.step}, Level: {current_frame.level}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
print()
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['M', 'G', 'O', 'p', 'W'] and n['pixels'] >= 12:
        rs = [x for x,_ in n['boundary']]
        cs = [y for _,y in n['boundary']]
        center = ((min(rs)+max(rs))//2, (min(cs)+max(cs))//2)
        children = [seg['nodes'][c]['color'] for c in n['children']] if n['children'] else []
        print(f"color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} center={center} children={children}")

# Compare with previous frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    prev_squares = [(n['color'], n['boundary'][0]) for n in prev_seg['nodes'] if n['pixels'] == 20]
    curr_squares = [(n['color'], n['boundary'][0]) for n in seg['nodes'] if n['pixels'] == 20]
    print(f"\nPrev squares: {prev_squares}")
    print(f"Curr squares: {curr_squares}")
    
    prev_w = [(n['pixels'], n['boundary'][0]) for n in prev_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 20]
    curr_w = [(n['pixels'], n['boundary'][0]) for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 20]
    print(f"Prev white small: {prev_w}")
    print(f"Curr white small: {curr_w}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
